Title: IOI Corp's Q3 earnings rise 8.0pct to RM283.3mil on lower revenue
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: IOI Corp Bhd posted an eight per cent rise in net profit for the third quarter ended March 31, 2026, to RM283.3 million from RM262.3 million a year ago.
This was mainly driven by stronger contributions from the plantation and resource-based manufacturing segments.
IOI' Corp's revenue, however, dropped 2.1 per cent to RM2.67 billion from RM2.73 billion a year earlier, its Bursa Malaysia filing showed.
No dividend was declared for the quarter.
For the nine-month period, its net profit grew 8.9 per cent to RM1.18 billion from RM1.08 billion a year earlier, while revenue rose 4.3 per cent to RM8.73 billion against RM8.37 billion previously.
IOI Corp said crude palm oil (CPO) prices are expected to remain supported by elevated soybean oil prices, which continue to trade at a significant premium to CPO, as well as higher biodiesel mandates in Malaysia and Indonesia.
However, it said the upside could be limited by seasonally stronger fresh fruit bunch production.
This may lead to higher palm oil inventory levels, coupled with softer demand from key importing countries.
"In addition, Indonesia's recently announced plan to centralise CPO exports introduces uncertainty and may amplify near term price volatility.
"On balance, while volatility is likely to persist in the near term, CPO price is anticipated to range between RM4,300 and RM4,600 per tonne over the next three months," it said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Innovation is no longer the CEO's job alone
Article:
Malaysia has long spoken about becoming an innovation-driven nation. Yet in many organisations, innovation still sits within a strategy unit, research department, or the minds of senior executives.
We continue to assume that breakthroughs emerge from boardrooms rather than from employees closest to the daily realities of customers and operations.
But what if the next transformative idea comes from a customer service officer struggling with inefficient systems? Or from a junior employee who notices what customers are too frustrated to articulate?
The future of innovation may not belong to the loudest voices at the top. It may belong to organisations that learn how to listen better. This is where Design Thinking deserves far greater attention in Malaysia's workplaces.
Design Thinking is often misunderstood as a trendy workshop activity involving sticky notes and brainstorming sessions. In reality, it is a powerful human-centred approach to problemsolving that encourages organisations to deeply understand people before creating solutions.
It begins with empathy, not assumptions. It values experimentation over perfection. Most importantly, it democratises innovation. The urgency for this shift is becoming increasingly clear.
According to Gallup's State of the Global Workplace report, only 23 per cent of employees worldwide are actively engaged at work. Disengaged employees are far less likely to contribute ideas, challenge inefficiencies, or participate meaningfully in innovation efforts.
Meanwhile, a 2023 IBM Institute for Business Value report identified employee resistance to change as one of the biggest barriers to digital transformation. Innovation is no longer just about technology. It is increasingly about culture.
For decades, many organisations operated through rigid hierarchies where employees were expected to execute instructions rather than shape ideas. Today, that model is becoming outdated. Markets move too quickly.
Customer expectations evolve too rapidly. Leaders alone cannot possibly identify every operational inefficiency, customer frustration, or emerging opportunity.
Employees can.
Frontline employees often understand customer pain points better than senior management because they encounter them daily. Yet many organisations fail to tap into this collective intelligence because employees fear speaking up.
Mistakes are punished, hierarchy dominates discussion, and innovation becomes associated with risk rather than learning. A 2024 McKinsey report found that organisations with high levels of psychological safety were significantly more likely to outperform peers in innovation outcomes.
Creativity cannot thrive where people are afraid to fail. This is why Design Thinking matters beyond innovation itself. It creates environments where employees feel safe asking questions, challenging assumptions, and testing ideas without fear of immediate judgment.
Organisations move away from a culture of control toward a culture of curiosity. This shift is particularly important for Malaysia as we navigate economic uncertainty, digital transformation, and rising global competition. Improving productivity today requires more than efficiency measures alone.
It requires organisations to rethink how ideas are generated and how employees contribute to value creation. However, innovation is not just about generating more ideas. It is also about identifying which problems are worth solving.
Many companies focus only on existing customers and familiar markets. Yet some of the most disruptive innovations emerge from overlooked groups whose frustrations are ignored.
Successful organisations pay attention to weak signals, hidden frustrations, and underserved needs long before competitors do.
Malaysia needs more organisations willing to think this way. At the same time, businesses must learn to balance stability with experimentation.
They still need operational discipline and reliable delivery, but they also need room for bold thinking. Research by Deloitte found that companies with strong innovation cultures are twice as likely to achieve above-average revenue growth compared to competitors.
This balance is becoming even more critical as artificial intelligence and automation rapidly reshape industries. The World Economic Forum estimates that nearly 44 per cent of workers' skills will be disrupted within the next five years.
Employees are no longer simply adapting to change. Increasingly, they must become co-creators of change. But empowering employees requires more than motivational slogans. Leaders must redesign systems to support employee-driven innovation.
This includes creating time for experimentation, encouraging cross-functional collaboration, rewarding initiative, and accepting that not every idea will succeed.
Failure must stop being viewed as incompetence and start being viewed as learning. Equally important is leadership behaviour. Employees quickly notice whether leaders genuinely welcome new ideas or merely claim to.
A single dismissive response can silence future creativity. Conversely, leaders who openly invite participation create trust. Trust is the real foundation of innovation.
Malaysia has immense human talent across industries, universities, SMEs, startups, and public institutions. The challenge is not whether Malaysians are creative enough. The challenge is whether our organisations are designed to unlock that creativity.
Innovation should no longer belong to a select few at the top. The organisations that thrive in the future will be those that recognise innovation as a collective capability. The next breakthrough idea may already exist within the organisation.
The real question is whether anyone is listening.
Associate Professor Dr Jesrina Ann Xavier
Department of Management, Sunway Business School,
Sunway University
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: WTK's unit to dispose of shares in Biogrow City Plantations to Rimbun Temasek
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Sarawak-based diversified group WTK Holdings Bhd's units Biogrow City Sdn Bhd (BCSB) and Bioworld Synergies Sdn Bhd (BSSB) have proposed to sell their interest and preference shares in Biogrow City Plantations Sdn Bhd (BCPSB) to Rimbun Temasek Sdn Bhd for RM90 million.
In a Bursa Malaysia filing today, it said the proposed disposals comprise BCSB's disposal of 8.5 million ordinary shares, representing an 85 per cent equity interest in BCPSB, for RM65.6 million, and BSSB's disposal of 24.4 million redeemable preference shares in BCPSB for RM24.4 million.
"The proposed disposals are expected to result in an estimated gain of RM72.7 million to the group," it said, adding that BCPSB will cease to be a subsidiary of BCSB upon completion by the fourth quarter of 2026.
WTK executive director Francis Lai said the proposed disposals provide the group with an opportunity to unlock value from underperforming assets that are geographically distant from its main plantation clusters, as plantations remain a key business segment for WTK.
However, he said Biogrow's plantation assets have been challenging to optimise due to their location, terrain, and the limited availability of surrounding third-party fresh crops to support mill utilisation.
"Hence, the proposed disposals will allow us to further enhance our plantation portfolio performance, especially following the earlier addition of two oil palm plantations and one palm oil mill in April 2026 that have better yields and production output.
"The proceeds of RM90 million will strengthen our balance sheet, improve our financial flexibility and provide additional working capital as we continue to build a more resilient and sustainable earnings base," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: IJM Corp unit to sell Sabah property to Econsave-linked company for RM47.5mil
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: IJM Corporation Bhd's (IJM Corp) indirect wholly-owned subsidiary, IJM Properties Sdn Bhd, has signed a sale and purchase agreement (SPA) to dispose of a piece of land together with a single-storey building in Sandakan, Sabah, to Coupang Sdn Bhd for RM47.5 million in cash.
Coupang is the asset-holding company of Econsave Cash & Carry Sdn Bhd (Econsave Group).
In a filing with Bursa Malaysia today, IJM Corp said the proposed disposal of the 9,518.4-square metre land along with the building is deemed a related-party transaction under Bursa Malaysia's Main Market Listing Requirements in view of the interests of one of its directors in the deal.
"The proposed disposal presents an opportunity to unlock value and redeploy capital more efficiently, while improving the company's liquidity position, strengthening cash flow and optimising capital allocation in line with the group's strategic objectives," it said.
The group said the disposal consideration was determined on a willing-buyer willing-seller basis, taking into account the property's valuation of RM33.6 million as at March 31, 2025, as well as its rental yield for the remaining lease period.
IJM Corp also said the proceeds from the proposed disposal are intended primarily for working capital requirements, with RM46 million earmarked for the purpose within 24 months of the disposal's completion.
The remaining RM1.5 million will be used to meet expenses related to the proposed disposal.
It said the proposed disposal is subject to the fulfilment of the terms and conditions of the SPA, adding that any failure to comply with the agreement's terms, warranties and undertakings could result in its termination.
"Nevertheless, the board will endeavour to take all necessary steps to ensure that all SPA terms and actions required to be undertaken are fulfilled in a timely manner to facilitate the completion of the proposed disposal," it said.
The proposed disposal is expected to be completed by the third quarter of 2026, IJM Corp said.
It added that the proposed sale will not affect its share capital or the shareholdings of its substantial shareholders as the transaction does not involve the issuance of new shares.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor BN reps confirm receiving notice for June 22 sitting
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Several Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) assemblymen have confirmed receiving notices to attend a state assembly sitting scheduled for June 22.
The assemblymen disclosed this after attending the Johor BN meeting at the Johor Umno Liaison Committee office here today.
Sri Medan assemblyman Datuk Zulkurnain Kamisan confirmed receiving the notice this morning but said he was unaware of the agenda for the sitting.
"The letter informed us about the state assembly sitting, but I do not know if it is related to dissolution or otherwise. However, if they want to dissolve the state assembly, a sitting is not necessary," he said.
He said based on the experience of the 2022 Johor state election, the dissolution announcement was made directly by the then menteri besar Datuk Seri Hasni Mohammad.
"If we look at the 2022 election, the announcement of the dissolution of the state assembly was made by the Johor menteri besar right here at the Johor Umno Liaison Committee office," he said.
Yong Peng assemblyman Ling Tian Soon, who is also the state Health and Environment Committee chairman, and Paloh assemblyman Lee Ting Han, who is the state Investment, Trade, Consumer Affairs and Human Resources Committee chairman, also confirmed receiving the same notice.
On April 30, the Regent of Johor, Tunku Mahkota Ismail, officiated the first meeting of the fifth session of the 15th Johor state legislative assembly at Bangunan Sultan Ismail, Iskandar Puteri.
Speculation regarding a Johor state election has intensified following a leaked message about a special state assembly sitting allegedly scheduled for June 24 to dissolve the state assembly.
However, Johor state assembly deputy speaker Datuk Samsolbari Jamali said on May 22 that the special sitting had not been confirmed.
This morning, Johor Jaya assemblyman Liow Cai Tung shared the
meeting notice
on social media, sparking further speculation about a potential dissolution.
She questioned the actual agenda of the June 22 meeting, noting that it would last only one day and comes shortly after last month's sitting.
She also raised questions as the meeting notice stated that no motions or questions would be accepted.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Wan Saiful: Hamzah has moved on, never plotted to split Bersatu
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Tasek Gelugor member of parliament Datuk Wan Saiful Wan Jan defended former Bersatu deputy president Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin against claims that he sought to divide the party.
He dismissed allegations by Datuk Dr Marzuki Mohamad, a former political secretary to Bersatu president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin, who said Hamzah had used Pas, Umno and the royal institution to split Bersatu.
He said it was a baseless attempt to cover up the weaknesses of certain quarters in the party.
"Hamzah has moved on from Bersatu. He no longer speaks about the party.
"He's now focused on the Reset agenda," Wan Saiful said.
Marzuki had claimed that a former pro-Hamzah Bersatu MP uploaded a picture on Facebook showing a meeting between Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang, Hamzah and several Bersatu and Pas MPs at the so-called "White House", Hamzah's office in Bukit Tunku.
Wan Saiful, formerly Bersatu information chief, said he had attended the Feb 18 meeting last year, which was also attended by several senior Bersatu and Pas leaders.
They included Bersatu vice-president Datuk Dr Radzi Jidin, former Bersatu supreme council member Datuk Seri Saifuddin Abdullah, and former Bersatu youth chief Wan Ahmad Fayhsal Wan Ahmad Kamal.
"If Radzi has any dignity, he should confirm what took place at the meeting. Do not let Marzuki speak out of ignorance.
"Pas was not born yesterday. Pas has governed states and has experience at the federal level. Accusing Hamzah or anyone else of using Pas proves that they are suffering from a serious denial syndrome.
"If Pas wants to reassess its relationship with Bersatu, it is because of the wrong advice given by those around Muhyiddin," he said in a Facebook post today.
Yesterday, Marzuki claimed that
Hamzah had used Pas and Umno
as political capital to divide Bersatu and take over the party from Muhyiddin.
Wan Saiful claimed Muhyiddin's relationship with the palace was not warm, including over issues linked to a press conference on the rejection of the unity government proposal previously.
He also claimed Hamzah had played an important role in securing the support of Pas and Umno MPs, which led to Muhyiddin being appointed the eighth prime minister in 2020.
He said Hamzah had also helped fund the party when he was Bersatu secretary-general, using his own money to ensure the smooth running of Bersatu and Perikatan Nasional operations.
"Marzuki is also trying to portray Hamzah as having become deputy president only because of Muhyiddin's endorsement.
"I was in the supreme council then. My reading is that Muhyiddin actually wanted to save Bersatu vice president Datuk Seri Ahmad Faizal Azumu.
"Muhyiddin knew that if there was a contest, Faizal would lose badly. Muhyiddin did not want to lose Faizal because he needed his support.
"Hamzah did not need Muhyiddin's endorsement because even then, he had his own strength. The proof is that after he was sacked, 18 MPs, many assemblymen and more than 120 division chiefs chose to be with him," he claimed.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ringgit ends higher against US dollar on renewed risk appetite
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit ended the week higher against the US dollar on Friday, supported by renewed risk appetite following reports of a possible reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, a key global oil shipping route.
At 6pm, the local currency rose to 3.9625/9670 against the greenback from Thursday's close of 3.9770/9805.
Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid told Bernama that the development boosted investor confidence and eased concerns over potential disruptions to global oil supplies.
He said that, as a result, crude oil prices retreated, with West Texas Intermediate (WTI) and Brent crude trading around US$88 and US$93 per barrel, respectively.
Back home, the ringgit also traded higher against a basket of major currencies.
It rose against the British pound to 5.3165/3225 from 5.3272/3319 at Thursday's close, improved against the euro to 4.6127/6180 from 4.6181/6222, and increased versus the Japanese yen to 2.4874/4904 from yesterday's close of 2.4947/4970.
Meanwhile, the local currency traded mostly higher against regional peers.
It appreciated versus the Singapore dollar to 3.1010/1048 from 3.1085/1115 at Thursday's close, but eased against the Thai baht to 12.1732/1926 from 12.1613/1773 previously.
The ringgit was higher against the Indonesian rupiah at 221.6/221.9 versus 222.8/223.1 previously and was firmer compared with the Philippine peso to 6.43/6.44, from 6.45/6.46 at Thursday's close.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Japan spent US$73bil in yen interventions
Article:
TOKYO: The Bank of Japan spent around 11.7 trillion yen (US$73 billion) in the past month to prop up the yen, official government data showed on Friday.
The central bank intervenes in the market under the instruction of the finance ministry in an attempt to ease economic damage from sharp fluctuations in exchange rates.
The data released by the finance ministry showed that the market interventions happened between April 28 and May 27, but did not specify exact dates.
The interventions reportedly began on April 30 when the Japanese currency weakened to near 160 yen per dollar, the lowest in almost two years.
Japanese media reported on May 8 that the bank had by then spent 10 trillion yen propping up the currency.
There have since been several spikes in the currency through to early May, touching 155 on May 6, sparking speculation of further moves by authorities.
The Japanese unit was back around 159.2 to the dollar on Friday, prompting questions about the effectiveness of the interventions.
The yen has weakened on the back of the recent rise in oil prices caused by the Middle East war, as well as the gap between US and Japanese interest rates.
The last time Japanese authorities intervened in the market was in July 2024, when the yen neared 162 per dollar, spending some 5.5 trillion yen.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Guan Chong's net profit rose 30pct to RM123.58mil in Q1
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Guan Chong Bhd's net profit rose 30.6 per cent to RM123.58 million in the first quarter ended March 31, 2026 (Q1 FY26) from RM94.6 million a year ago.
In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, the company said the higher earnings posted during the quarter was due to lower finance costs arising from lower borrowing.
However, the company's revenue slipped 39.1 per cent to RM2.62 billion  in Q1 FY26 from RM4.31 billion in the same period last year dragged down by lower selling price for cocoa products.
The company declared a second interim dividend of 1.5 sen per share for the financial year ending Dec 31, 2026, to be paid on July 10, 2026.
Looking ahead, the company said cocoa prices are expected to remain relatively stable following the significant correction from last year's highs. This is further supported by improved supply from better weather in current crops and maturing plantings in key producing regions.
"Demand is expected to recover in the second half of 2026, particularly in Q4, as declining cocoa prices translate into lower chocolate prices, making products more accessible and encouraging consumers to resume or increase purchases," the company said.
Over the longer term, the company said that the structural demand outlook remains positive, supported by the ongoing shift towards real chocolate reformulation as manufacturers move away from substitute ingredients.
"However, uncertainty rising from the ongoing Middle East conflict could weigh on regional demand and broader consumer sentiment in the near term. Further, the anticipated El Niño is expected to tighten cocoa supply in the upcoming crop cycle.
"The group remains committed to its core cocoa ingredient processing business while strategically expanding into the industrial chocolate segment and optimising production in line with evolving market conditions," it added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MARKET PULSE PM MAY 29, 2026 [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia ended lower on Friday, extending its losses despite easing geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, although overall market breadth remained positive.
The benchmark index closed at 1,683, lifted by the healthcare sector while the energy sector recorded the steepest decline.
Among the most actively traded counters at the close were Axiata Bhd, YTL Corp Bhd and Mr DIY Group (M) Bhd.
Meanwhile, the ringgit strengthened slightly against the US dollar, closing at 3.9660.
Bitcoin was trading higher at RM292,528, while Ethereum rose to RM7,972.
That wraps up today's Market Pulse.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Inta Bina bags RM227.08mil mixed commercial development contract in Klang
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Inta Bina Group Bhd has secured a RM227.08 million contract from Ancubic PJ Sdn Bhd for the proposed main building and infrastructure works for a mixed commercial development in Klang, Selangor.
In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, the construction company said the large-scale strata commercial development is scheduled to commence on June 15, 2026, and is expected to be completed within 36 months.
"The development comprises two phases featuring serviced apartments, retail podiums, parking facilities, lifestyle amenities and related infrastructure works.
"Upon completion, the project will deliver 976 serviced apartments and Rumah Mampu Milik units, alongside commercial and community-oriented facilities," it said.
Following the contract award, Inta Bina's unfilled order book stands at approximately RM1.7 billion, including the newly secured project.
The group added that it recorded revenue of RM750.6 million for the financial year 2025, reflecting the strength of its project pipeline and execution capabilities.
Inta Bina managing director Paul Lim said the contract strengthens the group's order book and reinforces its presence in the Klang Valley construction market.
"We are pleased to work with Ancubic Group, a new client for Inta Bina, contributing to the group's KPI of securing at least one new client per year.
"The appointment of Inta Bina as the main contractor reflects continued demand for the group's construction expertise in integrated developments," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia, Saudi advance cooperation in security and enforcement
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia and Saudi Arabia are deepening security and enforcement ties, with both countries finalising key areas of cooperation ranging from border control to counter-terrorism under the Saudi-Malaysia Coordination Council (SMCC).
Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said progress on the matters was discussed during a meeting with Saudi Arabia's Interior Minister Prince Abdulaziz bin Saud bin Nayef bin Abdulaziz Al-Saud after he performed the haj pilgrimage this year.
He said ties between Malaysia and Saudi Arabia continued to be strengthened through the SMCC, which serves as the main platform to coordinate various bilateral initiatives.
"Through the SMCC Security Cluster, Malaysia and Saudi Arabia have finalised seven key areas of cooperation covering border security, counter-terrorism, cyber security, immigration, forensics, search and rescue operations, and efforts to combat narcotics crimes," he said in a Facebook post today.
He said progress had also been made through the Committee of Experts, which reached an agreement to implement 10 strategic initiatives involving various security and enforcement agencies from both countries.
Saifuddin said the development reflected the commitment of Malaysia and Saudi Arabia to strengthen expertise sharing, intelligence exchange and enforcement cooperation in facing increasingly complex cross-border security challenges.
He added that Prince Abdulaziz had expressed appreciation for Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim's role and leadership at the international level, particularly in promoting dialogue, diplomacy and understanding as the best path to ease conflict and preserve global peace.
Saifuddin also thanked the Saudi government for its efforts, commitment and improvements in managing the haj pilgrimage, particularly for Malaysian pilgrims.
He said the facilities, services and efficient management system had helped not only Malaysian pilgrims but also millions of Muslims from around the world perform their pilgrimage in a more comfortable and orderly manner.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor assemblyman dismisses election talk over one-day sitting
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Speculation surrounding a possible dissolution of the Johor state assembly following the announcement of a one-day sitting on June 22 should be stopped to avoid unnecessary tension and political perception.
DAP Skudai assemblyman Marina Ibrahim said the brief state assembly sitting was not unprecedented and should not automatically be interpreted as a precursor to fresh state elections.
"It is not the first time a one-day sitting has happened in the past," she said when contacted today.
She said there was no basis for political speculation at this stage, especially as the dissolution of the state assembly ultimately rests on the consent of the Sultan of Johor.
"If the state assembly is to be dissolved, there is actually no need for a sitting to be held.
"The absolute prerogative lies with the Sultan of Johor's consent, so why speculate and create unnecessary tension, speculation and perception?" she said.
She said the upcoming sitting could simply involve the tabling or approval of new policy matters.
"It might just be a sitting to approve certain new policies. Personally, I do not know why the House has been called, but as an elected assemblyman, I will attend it," she said.
Marina said her focus remained on serving the rakyat regardless of any political developments.
"My responsibility is to the people, especially those in my constituency, and I will continue to work even if the state assembly is dissolved," she added.
Her remarks came amid growing political chatter after notices were issued for a one-day Johor state assembly sitting scheduled for June 22 at Kota Iskandar.
The sitting, set to begin at 9.30am, will reportedly proceed without the usual oral question-and-answer session, an arrangement that has sparked speculation among political observers and opposition figures.
Earlier, DAP Johor Jaya member of parliament Liow Cai Tung questioned the unusual format after sharing the official invitation letter on Facebook, noting that the sitting was limited to a
single day
and excluded oral questions.
Liow also pointed out that a state assembly could technically be dissolved without convening a sitting, fuelling further speculation over whether the session could signal broader political movement in Johor.
The invitation letter issued by the Johor state legislative assembly secretary stated that no questions would be accepted for the sitting, while the order of business would be circulated later.
Several Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) assemblymen contacted declined to speculate on the matter but confirmed they had received invitations to attend the sitting.
Johor Menteri Besar and Johor BN chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi had earlier said the state was prepared to
contest all 56 state seats
in the next election.
The current state administration, led by BN, secured a two-thirds majority in the 2022 Johor polls after winning 40 of the 56 seats.
The next Johor state election must be held by April next year unless the state assembly is dissolved earlier.
Meanwhile, MCA president Datuk Seri Dr Wee Ka Siong said a Johor state election could be called at any time after more than four years of BN administration, adding that voters would ultimately judge the government's performance since 2022 based on economic growth, investment inflows, job creation and statewide development benefits.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Finas: Screenings of local film 'Konspirasi' halted at publisher's request
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The nationwide screening of local film Konspirasi has been halted since May 28 at the request of its publisher, the National Film Development Corporation Malaysia (Finas) said.
Finas said it was informed of the matter today by the Malaysian Association of Film Exhibitors (MAFE) and distributor Media Prima Bhd.
Based on an email shared with Finas, publisher Elepfilm Entertainment Sdn Bhd requested that screenings be stopped from that date.
"Checks with the distributor and cinema operators found that the termination of screenings was made at the publisher's own request and did not involve any directive from any party," Finas said.
The agency said Konspirasi had obtained approval from the Film Censorship Board (LPF) and was approved under the Mandatory Screening Scheme on May 14.
The film began screening on May 21 across 120 cinema screens nationwide.
Finas added that it has attempted to contact the publisher for further explanation but has yet to receive a response.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Cabnet unit bags RM38.7mil data centre containment system contract
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Cabnet Holdings Bhd's (CHB) wholly-owned subsidiary, Cabnet Systems (M) Sdn Bhd (CSM), has secured a RM38.68 million contract from Unique Arena Sdn Bhd for the supply and installation of a data centre containment system in Selangor.
In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, CHB said CSM accepted the purchase order from Unique Arena today.
The company said the contract works are scheduled to commence on May 29, 2026, and are expected to be completed by Nov 30, 2026.
"The contract works are expected to contribute positively to the group's earnings and net assets per share over the duration of the contract works.
"The contract works will not have any impact on the share capital and shareholding structure of the company," CHB said.
On potential risks, the company said the project is subject to the usual operational and execution risks encountered in the normal course of business, adding that it would take appropriate measures to mitigate and minimise such risks.
It also noted that none of its directors, major shareholders, or persons connected to them has any interest, whether direct or indirect, in the contract awarded, while the board was of the opinion that the acceptance of the contract works is in the ordinary course of business and in the best interests of CHB.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: TNB issues RM4bil sustainability sukuk wakalah
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Tenaga Nasional Bhd (TNB) has issued RM4 billion worth of Sustainability Sukuk Wakalah under its Sukuk Wakalah Programme.
In a Bursa Malaysia filing today, the electric utility company stated that the issuance, the first under the programme, comprised five tranches.
"The tranches include: RM450 million with a seven-year tenure carrying a periodic distribution rate of 3.81 per cent per annum; RM300 million with a 10-year tenure at 3.93 per cent; RM850 million with a 15-year tenure at 4.19 per cent; RM1.05 billion with a 20-year tenure at 4.31 per cent; and RM1.35 billion with a 25-year tenure at 4.37 per cent," said TNB.
Maybank Investment Bank Bhd and CIMB Investment Bank Bhd are acting as joint principal advisers and joint lead arrangers for the Sukuk Wakalah Programme, as well as joint lead managers for the RM4 billion issuance.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Tonton booth draws crowds at PBAKL 2026 with exclusive merchandise, streaming deals
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Tonton booth at the Kuala Lumpur International Book Fair 2026 (PBAKL 2026) is drawing large crowds with its exclusive limited-edition merchandise, special subscription packages, and family-friendly activities.
Organised through a collaboration between Tonton and Media Prima Television Networks, the booth at the World Trade Centre Kuala Lumpur (WTCKL) offers visitors special promotional rates available only during the 10-day event.
TV3, TV9, and Tonton activation content marketing senior executive Muhammad Khairol Anuar Zawawi said visitors who subscribe to Tonton packages during the fair will receive premium gifts.
These include limited-edition merchandise from the popular animated series Ejen Ali and the drama Yes! Captain Zul Aaryan.
"Normally, when people subscribe online, they only receive the subscription package. But during the book fair, subscribers can receive exclusive merchandise valued higher than the subscription price itself," he said.
Khairol said the booth is targeting both parents and students, aligning with the theme of this year's fair, "Malaysia Membaca: Merayakan Buku".
"For parents looking for entertainment, we offer Tonton packages, while students can subscribe to TV-Tuisyen packages. Both packages are available from RM9.90 per month," he said, adding that more than 20 subscriptions were recorded within the first three hours of the fair opening.
"We are targeting about 2,500 subscriptions throughout the 10-day event. There are different subscription options, including one-month, three-month, and one-year packages, with unique gifts for each," he said.
Apart from subscription promotions, the booth is featuring special activities today, including a Tonton colouring contest, mascot appearances by Ejen Ali characters, TV-Tuisyen Live sessions, and the Tonton Chess King of the Ring competition.
Meanwhile, Ejen Ali merchandise booth staff member Nurul Aishah said special collectables were introduced in conjunction with the animated series's 10th anniversary celebration.
Among the items available are anniversary pins, exclusive T-shirts, caps, and novels featuring the cast's signatures.
She noted that some of the items, including the limited-edition Satria Cero shirts, are not widely available outside selected roadshows.
"We also have special combo sets for fans of Yes! Captain Zul Aaryan, including novels and exclusive merchandise," she said.
Customer, Naziruzzaman Nazlli, 40, who visited the Tonton booth, subscribed to a three-month Tonton package of RM28 today.
She said the platform was very convenient.
"It is easier now because people can watch through their mobile phones. I like watching romantic dramas, and it is more convenient because everything can be streamed faster online," he said.
PBAKL 2026 is being held at WTCKL from May 29 to June 7, open from 10am to 10pm daily.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Buffalo attack survivor in Johor recalls terrifying ordeal
Article:
JOHOR BARU: A Felda Ulu Tebrau supervisor sustained severe injuries after being gored and tossed twice by a one-tonne buffalo during a Hari Raya Aidiladha korban programme in Kampung Sungai Tiram, Ulu Tiram, on Wednesday.
Shaifirol Shahlan, 45, suffered three broken ribs on his left side, wounds to his stomach and scratches on several parts of his body in the 1pm incident.
"I came to watch the slaughter process and did not expect to be gored by the buffalo," he said when met at his home in Batu 20, Kampung Sungai Tiram, today.
Shaifirol said he was grateful that he was still able to get up and escape after the attack, which he said helped prevent more serious injuries.
In pain and experiencing shortness of breath, his friends rushed him to the Ulu Tiram health clinic before he was referred to Sultan Ismail Hospital (HSI).
"During the incident, I was on the right side of the slaughterhouse and saw the buffalo acting aggressively while being pulled away.
"I did not expect the buffalo to head towards me. I tried to jump to save myself, but my leg got caught in the buffalo rope. I fell, and the buffalo gored and tossed me twice," he said.
He received treatment for two nights at HSI and was discharged at 10pm yesterday.
The father of three, who has been given two weeks of medical leave, said he did not expect the incident to go viral after footage was shared online by bystanders.
Despite his injuries, Shaifirol said he was not traumatised and accepted the incident as fate. He remains determined to help residents with future sacrificial and aqiqah rituals.
"Now I can only walk slowly and have to sit on a high chair. When sleeping, I cannot lie down normally and must elevate the mattress," he said.
His wife, Noorain Mohd Jabir, said she was cooking when she was informed of the incident. She rushed to HSI to accompany her husband.
Separately, a man was reported
killed after being gored
by a buffalo during a sacrificial ritual in Kampung Tenang, Ulu Tiram, located about 2km away on the same day.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Late selling drags Bursa Malaysia to close marginally lower
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia reversed earlier gains to close marginally lower on Friday, pressured by late selling in blue-chip stocks as investors turned cautious amid ongoing geopolitical uncertainties in West Asia, which continued to dampen market sentiment.
At 5pm, the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) eased 1.86 points, or 0.11 per cent, to 1,683.07 from Thursday's close of 1,684.93.
The benchmark index opened 3.25 points higher at 1,688.18. and moved between 1,680.59 and 1,694.65 throughout the session.
However, the broader market was positive, with gainers outpacing losers 680 to 512, while 529 counters were unchanged, 921 untraded, and 51 suspended.
Turnover rose to 4.94 billion units valued at RM10.74 billion compared with 3.53 billion units valued at RM4.19 billion on Thursday.
Rakuten Trade Sdn Bhd vice-president of equity research, Thong Pak Leng, said regional markets rebounded amid growing optimism that the United States and Iran could reach a peace agreement, easing concerns over potential disruptions to global energy supplies.
"Consequently, Brent crude oil prices declined to around US$92 per barrel.
"However, back home, we remain cautious as negotiations between the US and Iran have yet to yield a definitive outcome, while geopolitical uncertainties in West Asia continue to weigh on investor confidence," he told Bernama.
Among the heavyweights, Maybank gained 14 sen to RM10.64, Tenaga Nasional added four sen to RM14.28, and IHH Healthcare edged up two sen to RM9.01, while Public Bank fell six sen to RM4.71 and CIMB Group slipped one sen to RM7.48.
Among the active stocks, Axiata perked up two sen to RM2.00, YTL Corp and MR D.I.Y. were one sen higher at RM2.05 and RM1.58, respectively, QL Resources jumped 20 sen to RM3.60, and NationGate Holdings soared 12 sen to 88 sen.
Leading gainers included Hong Leong Industries, which surged 62 sen to RM19.00, United Plantations and Ajinomoto rose 58 sen each to RM31.08 and RM13.86, respectively, and Petronas Dagangan climbed 40 sen to RM17.74, and Kuala Lumpur Kepong put on 36 sen to RM20.32.
Among the top losers, Nestle sank RM4.00 to RM91.00,  Allianz Malaysia dipped 40 sen to RM21.52, Malayan Cement dropped 35 sen to RM6.54, while Hong Leong Bank and Petronas Chemicals each lost 30 sen to RM20.88 and RM5.21, respectively.
On the index board, the FBM Emas Index gained 38.84 points to 12,580.12, the FBM Top 100 Index increased 37.06 points to 12,417.54, and the FBM Emas Shariah Index added 53.16 points to 12,604.07.
The FBM Mid 70 Index surged 270.64 points to 18,484.33 and the FBM ACE Index advanced 42.67 points to 4,763.81.
By sector, the Industrial Products and Services Index shed 0.51 of a point to 197.82, the Financial Services Index fell 21.72 points to 19,524.29, the Plantation Index slid 5.41 points to 8,499.54, and the Energy Index eased 4.15 points to 778.75.
The Main Market volume expanded to 3.56 billion units valued at RM10.15 billion from 1.98 billion units valued at RM3.87 billion on Thursday.
Warrants turnover slipped to 830.47 million units valued at RM105.18 million from 971.39 million units valued at RM117.20 million yesterday.
The ACE Market volume narrowed to 550.09 million units valued at RM218.76 million from 583.66 million units valued at RM206.61 million previously.
Consumer products and services counters accounted for 750.34 million shares traded on the Main Market, industrial products and services (502.46 million), construction (220.58 million), technology (342 million), financial services (296.28 million), property (230.92 million), plantation (61.57 million), real estate investment trusts (42.34 million), closed-end fund (65,000), energy (118.92 million), healthcare (198.96 million), telecommunications and media (403.70 million), transportation and logistics (26.58 million), utilities (362.81 million), and business trusts (32,200).
Bursa Malaysia will be closed on June 1 and 2 for the King's Birthday and Wesak Day holidays, and will resume operations on June 3.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: U Mobile sets new national standards for indoor connectivity
Article:
RECENT
Opensignal
findings showed improvements in U Mobile's indoor 5G connectivity and network consistency following the rollout of its independently operated ULTRA5G network.
The report comes months after U Mobile launched its own 5G infrastructure in August last year, moving beyond the previous wholesale-based model under Digital Nasional Bhd (DNB).
The transition allows the company to manage its own network deployment and performance strategy, particularly in high-traffic indoor locations where connectivity is often more difficult to maintain.
According to Opensignal, more U Mobile users are now experiencing 5G download speeds alongside improvements in upload speeds and service consistency.
Over the past year, Opensignal data also recorded a 51 per cent improvement in 5G download speed and a 27 per cent increase in upload speed among U Mobile users.
INDOOR CONNECTIVITY
While outdoor coverage often dominates discussions around 5G, indoor performance remains one of the technology's biggest challenges due to physical barriers such as walls, glass and dense urban infrastructure.
Opensignal's findings showed that U Mobile users were twice as likely to experience an "Excellent" indoor 5G signal, while only eight per cent of indoor readings on U Mobile's network fell into the "Weak", "Very Weak" or "Dead Zone" categories.
The report attributed these improvements not only to spectrum deployment, but also to U Mobile's rollout of 5G In-Building Coverage (IBC) infrastructure across high-density locations.
This includes shopping malls, transport hubs and other indoor spaces where mobile traffic demand is typically concentrated.
STRONGER EXPERIENCE IN CROWDED LOCATIONS
Opensignal data further showed that U Mobile recorded 5G Consistent Quality scores above 90 per cent in major malls such as Pavilion Kuala Lumpur and Sunway Pyramid.
The metric measures how consistently a network can support everyday digital activities such as video streaming, app usage, messaging and mobile payments without interruptions or delays.
The trend was also observed at key transport locations including KL Sentral and Kuala Lumpur International Airport, where indoor mobile connectivity is increasingly important as users rely less on public WiFi networks.
The report also highlighted changing consumer habits as indoor 5G performance improves.
Since the launch of ULTRA5G, Opensignal observed indoor mobile data usage among U Mobile users rising from 56 per cent to 59 per cent , while reliance on WiFi declined.
At the same time, 5G's share of indoor mobile data consumption increased from 28 per cent to 36 per cent.
The findings suggest that stronger and more reliable indoor 5G connectivity is gradually reducing the need for users to switch to WiFi, particularly in high-traffic public spaces.
EXPANDING 5G ECOSYSTEM
The developments come as Malaysia transitions towards a dual-network 5G model, creating greater infrastructure-based competition among operators.
Beyond pricing, operators are now increasingly competing on network quality, consistency and user experience.
Backed by USD1 billion in secured funding,U Mobile has exceeded its target of 80 per cent population coverage earlier this year and achieved Malaysia's Book of Record for fastest 5G rollout.
The company has also signed a three-year wholesale agreement with Telekom Malaysia to provide end-to-end 5G services, potentially broadening access to its network infrastructure across the country.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ambulance driver charged with murder of girlfriend's 2-year-old son
Article:
TEMERLOH: A 29-year-old ambulance driver was charged in the magistrate's court here today with the murder of a two-year-old boy on May 11.
No plea was recorded from the accused, Muhammad Iqbal Fitri Mazlan, after the charge was read before magistrate Anis Adlina Abu Johan.
Murder cases fall under the jurisdiction of the High Court.
Iqbal was accused of murdering Mohamad Hayder Abdul Mukmin at a house in Taman Seberang Temerloh Indah 2A about 9am on May 11.
He was charged under Section 302 of the Penal Code, which carries the death penalty or imprisonment of between 30 and 40 years, as well as a minimum of 12 strokes of the rotan, upon conviction if not sentenced to death.
Deputy public prosecutor Adriana Azrin did not offer bail, as the charge is non-bailable. The accused was unrepresented.
The court fixed Sept 1 for mention.
The case was initially classified as abuse after the victim was brought unconscious to Sultan Haji Ahmad Shah Hospital on May 13.
It was later reclassified as murder after the post-mortem revealed that the victim died due to blunt force trauma to the head.
Police arrested the accused, who is also the victim's mother's boyfriend, along with the victim's mother in Temerloh on May 20.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police refer DUI case back to prosecution after rider dies in coma
Article:
KLANG: The police have referred the driving under the influence (DUI) case involving an unemployed man back to the prosecution for review after the food delivery rider he crashed into died following 13 days in a coma.
South Klang police chief Assistant Commissioner Lim Jit Huey confirmed the matter but said there have been no further developments so far.
The case comes after the death of
Zulfiqar Zamir Amirul Nizam
, 27, at the Tengku Ampuan Rahimah Hospital on Monday. He was due to get married in December.
On May 18, Low Kean Peng, 35, pleaded not guilty to driving under the influence of alcohol and causing serious injury to Zulfiqar.
Low was charged under Section 44(1A) of the Road Transport Act, which deals with driving under the influence of intoxicating liquor or drugs and causing injury to another person.
According to the charge, he allegedly drove a car with a blood alcohol level of 196mg per 100ml, exceeding the legal limit of 50mg per 100ml, and caused grievous injury to Zulfiqar.
The offence was allegedly committed at about 12.35am along Persiaran Kota on May 13.
At the time of the charge, Zulfiqar was in a coma at the intensive care unit due to severe injuries sustained in the impact after being struck by Low.
The magistrate granted bail at RM10,000 with one surety, suspended Low's driving licence and ordered him to report to the police station twice a month.
Meanwhile, Low's lawyer Ethan W.H. Tan did not rule out the possibility that the current charge may be revised following Zulfiqar's death.
He said he has not been informed by the prosecution or the police of any amended charge so far.
"I hope it will be maintained under the current provision instead of being changed to Section 302 of the Penal Code for murder.
"However, there is a possibility, but I have not received any updates, so the case remains status quo for now. We will wait for any notification from the police," he said.
He said if there is any amended charge, the prosecution may do so at the next mention on July 30.
He said Low is currently out on bail after it was posted by an uncle.
Zulfiqar, the eldest of four siblings, was on his way to deliver his final order of the night when he was allegedly struck by a car driven by the accused.
Lim had said the accused was believed to have been speeding while driving under the influence of alcohol.
He had allegedly driven against the traffic flow for 3.5km before colliding with Zulfiqar, who was riding his motorcycle.
The impact threw Zulfiqar Zamir from his motorcycle, leaving him with severe injuries.
Lim said the crash occurred when the accused, who was driving a Perodua Bezza, was travelling from Port Klang towards Shah Alam.
Police checks revealed that the accused had no record of prior criminal or drug-related offences.
Tests later confirmed that he was under the influence of alcohol at the time of the incident.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Decomposing body found in parked car near USM
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: A decomposing body, believed to be dead for five days, was found in a car opposite Universiti Sains Malaysia (USM) on Jalan Sungai Dua here today.
The grim discovery was made after passersby reported a foul smell coming from the parked vehicle.
When firefighters forced open the car door, they found the bloated body of a 37-year-old man slumped inside.
Jalan Perak Fire and Rescue station operations commander Shahrunnizam Kamar Bashah said firefighters were despatched to the scene after receiving a call at 12.28pm.
He added that the case has been handed over to the police for further investigation.
A nearby shop assistant said that the vehicle had been parked in the area for several days.
"I saw the car there for the past few days, but I never saw anyone inside or around it," she said.
The man's wife, also in her 30s, later identified the body at the scene.
She recalled the final moments before her husband disappeared, a late-night phone call that went unanswered.
She said her husband had suffered from diabetes.
He travelled to Kuala Lumpur last Wednesday for work and was supposed to return to Penang on Saturday.
He had told her after arriving in Penang that he needed to return to his office in Bayan Baru first.
"Between 11pm and midnight, I called him again after he failed to return home, but there was no answer.
"My concern grew after I failed to contact him the following day," she said, adding that she then lodged a police report.
Police later called to tell her that her husband's body had been found.
"I immediately rushed here, and my worst fear came true when I saw his body slumped inside the car," she added.
Northeast district deputy police chief Superintendent Lee Swee Sake said there were no signs of foul play, and the case has been classified as a sudden death report.
The deceased leaves behind his wife and two children.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Three Malaysian cadets complete training at elite US military service academies
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Three Malaysian cadets are among the latest to have completed their studies at the prestigious United States Military Academy at West Point and the United States Naval Academy (USNA), respectively.
The US Embassy in Kuala Lumpur, in its social media posts, extended congratulations to Cadet Edlan Mikyad Ahmad Syamly, who graduated from West Point, as well as Cadets Qarin Loqman Safriza and Mohamad Danish Imran Zainudin, who graduated from USNA.
"Graduating from one of the world's premier leadership institutions is a profound achievement. Edlan's journey reflects not only his personal grit and dedication but also the deep, enduring defence and educational partnerships between the United States and Malaysia.
"By training alongside international peers, leaders like Edlan foster global networks of cooperation, trust, and shared values that strengthen our nations' bilateral ties. We also commend his family, whose unwavering support — rain or shine — helped pave the way for this success.
"We wish Edlan the very best as he returns to serve Malaysia with distinction," it said.
Meanwhile, the embassy said that it has been an honour for the US Navy to host and train cadets Qarin and Danish.
[[nid:1451069]]
"Standing proud at the historic Navy-Marine Corps Memorial Stadium, these two exceptional young men have spent the last four years enduring some of the most rigorous academic, physical, and leadership training the US has to offer.
"A core mission of the US Service Academy international programme is equipping global talent with elite skills specifically so they can return home to lead.
"Qarin and Danish will soon head back to Malaysia, ready to apply their world-class Annapolis education and maritime training to their future service with the Royal Malaysian Navy," it said, adding that their success is a shining example of the deep, enduring US-Malaysia defence partnership.
It added that by investing in the next generation of military leaders together, the two nations continue to strengthen a shared commitment to regional security and a free, open Indo-Pacific.
The United States Military Academy at West Point, established in 1802, is a famed institution that trains and commissions officers for the US Army through a four-year programme combining academics, military instruction and leadership development.
The United States Naval Academy (USNA), established in 1845 in Annapolis, trains and commissions officers for the US Navy and Marine Corps through a four-year programme focused on academics, seamanship, engineering and leadership.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Media a strategic partner in ensuring country's stability, says Fadillah
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof has conveyed his appreciation to all journalists, media practitioners, and broadcasting personnel for their unwavering dedication to delivering accurate, authentic, and integrity-driven information.
In conjunction with National Journalists' Day (Hawana) 2026 today, he said journalists play a significant role in strengthening democracy and shaping a knowledgeable society, especially in an increasingly challenging and fast-changing media landscape.
"The role of journalists is not only to deliver news but also to act as an important bridge between the people and the government," he said in a post on his official Facebook account.
Fadillah said a free, responsible, and ethical media is a strategic partner of the government in ensuring Malaysia's stability, harmony, and continued progress.
He also praised the sacrifices of journalists who are constantly on the ground during disasters and crises to ensure the public receives balanced information.
He said in today's digital era, the spread of false information, slander, and sensationalism poses a challenge that requires media professionals to continue upholding the principles of journalism based on facts, integrity, and accountability.
He said the government always recognises the important role of media institutions in national development, adding that close synergy between both sides helps ensure the effective delivery of policies and development agendas to the people.
Fadillah expressed hope that the country's media industry would continue to evolve in line with technological advances without neglecting the values of ethics, professionalism, and humanity. Journalists are not only recorders of history, but also important individuals in shaping the mindset of society and the country's future, he said.
The Hawana 2026 celebration is scheduled to take place in Penang on June 20 with the theme "Media Integrity Strengthens Credibility".
The highlight of the annual event will be officiated by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim at the PICCA Convention Centre @ Arena Butterworth.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Subang MP urges PM's Dept to explain blocked access to MyKhas portal
Article:
SUBANG JAYA: Subang member of parliament Wong Chen has urged the Prime Minister's Department to provide a written explanation for the restriction of access to the MyKhas portal, which has remained unresolved for more than a week.
He said that despite multiple official communications, including hand-delivered letters to the Implementation Coordination Unit (ICU) under the Prime Minister's Department in Selangor and its headquarters in Putrajaya, no formal explanation had been given and access to the portal had yet to be restored.
"So what we hear from officers on the ground is a different story. I do not want to repeat it because it is hearsay, but they say this is a politically inspired move. That is all I am willing to say for now.
"If this 'punishment' is politically motivated, I would like to request the Prime Minister's Office to state so in writing, following which I shall seek the advice of my lawyers as to whether this constitutes an abuse of administrative power," he said in a press conference here today.
Asked whether the issue would affect his decision to stay in PKR, Wong said the matter had added pressure to an already difficult decision.
"The decision to stay in PKR is a difficult one. This one, of course, puts more pressure.
"A simple question I asked a lawyer friend is whether this constitutes constructive dismissal from the party, but we cannot say anything until we receive an official reply from the government. I do not think the prime minister is involved; it is too small a matter for him. We need an official response, then we will see," he added.
The portal, managed by the Implementation Coordination Unit under the Prime Minister's Department, is used to process constituency allocations and Projek Mesra Rakyat (PMR) applications.
Wong said if access continued to be withheld or no explanation was provided, the Prime Minister's Department should clarify whether his officers were expected to physically travel to Putrajaya to use government terminals to submit PMR applications for Subang residents.
He said government administrative systems and allocations exist to serve the rakyat, not to selectively disadvantage elected representatives carrying out their responsibilities to their constituents.
Wong said since access was denied on May 20, applications involving six schools had been affected, with planned projects unable to be uploaded.
He said People's Housing Project residents and underprivileged communities were also impacted, with delays affecting welfare initiatives, basic facilities, and assistance programmes aimed at easing cost-of-living pressures.
"Our office had also planned to assist two Pusat Pemulihan Dalam Komuniti centres for autistic children and persons with special needs through the development of sensory rooms.
"These initiatives are now delayed due to the blocked access," he said.
Wong said federal agencies, including the police and Fire and Rescue Department, were also affected, adding that delays in PMR applications would impact service delivery efficiency.
He said among the affected institutions were Surau Al-Mukminin in PJS 9, where roof repair works could not proceed, and the Sri Subramaniar Temple in PJS 7, which had also been impacted.
"What is deeply disappointing is that all these parties, schools, low-income communities, persons with disabilities centres, federal agencies and religious institutions continue to bear the consequences of an administrative restriction imposed on my parliamentary office without notice, without explanation, and without due process," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar voices concern over Gaza, southern Lebanon during Aidiladha
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today expressed deep concern over the situation facing Palestinians in Gaza and southern Lebanon as Muslims celebrate Hari Raya Aidiladha.
"During this Eid al-Adha celebration, we see Muslims, especially in Gaza and South Lebanon, being subjected to extreme Zionist Israeli oppression.
"The international community, particularly Europe and the United States, has failed to uphold justice, allowing continued suffering in the region," he said after performing prayers at the Saidina Hamzah mosque in Batu.
Anwar said he does not condone any harm against Jews, but stressed that the ongoing oppression of Palestinians must be condemned.
"What is dignity in this world? I want to emphasise the hypocrisy of the Western world. But I want to show our anger because this oppression continues," he said.
He said there must be wise and sane political leadership globally, and urged Malaysia's politics to remain focused on protecting people's welfare rather than being swayed by emotion.
"There is no resolution yet, including the continuous attacks on Iran and the blockade of Lebanon and Gaza.
"There should be an end to hostilities and international efforts to restore peace and dignity," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Do not blame mountain guides for hiker's disappearance, says rescued climber
Article:
TAPAH: Hiker Mohd Hanafi Neikmad, 41, has urged the public not to point fingers, especially at the forestry mountain guides (MGP), over the disappearance of missing hiker Jaslinda Saludin.
Hanafi and Jaslinda were among 14 participants and two MGPs who began the Trans Spencer Chapman trek via the Pos Gedung–Gunung Bah Gading–Gunung Batu Putih–Kuala Woh route at 2am on May 23.
He said the ordeal began on Saturday night when he was with Jaslinda and a mountain guide acting as the "sweeper", known as "Apis".
He said he was initially in the middle of the 14-member hiking group before stopping near Kem Batu Kor after experiencing chest tightness and vomiting, while his headlamp had also run out of battery amid rainy conditions.
According to him, the other 12 hikers had already moved far ahead, while he, Jaslinda and Apis waited until daylight on Sunday morning before continuing the climb to the summit of Gunung Batu Putih, the final peak in the three-peak expedition.
"About an hour after I sat by the trail, Apis passed by with Jaslinda. Seeing my condition, they brought me to Kem Batu Kor to rest overnight.
"Jaslinda said she wanted to continue the climb that same night because she was carpooling with other hikers. However, Apis advised against it because it was raining and I was suffering from chest pain and vomiting.
"So we rested at Kem Batu Kor until the next morning, but Jaslinda mentioned she wanted to leave earlier because she was relying on someone else's vehicle," he said when contacted today.
Hanafi was rescued on Monday through an airlift operation using the Fire and Rescue Department's AgustaWestland AW139 helicopter.
Following the incident, Hanafi was admitted to Tapah Hospital due to hypothermia, heart complications and low blood pressure before being transferred to Teluk Intan Hospital for further treatment until Monday.
Jaslinda, meanwhile, remains missing.
Recalling Sunday morning, Hanafi said Jaslinda and Apis headed toward the peak of Gunung Batu Putih while he waited at the camp.
Apis needed to reach the summit to secure a cellular signal to contact emergency services.
"I was too weak to continue and asked Apis to call the fire department. Jaslinda went with him to the peak, and Apis instructed her to stay put at the summit, dry her wet clothes, and strictly avoid moving, especially if she encountered a trail fork," Hanafi explained.
Hanafi added that Apis repeatedly trekked up and down the peak to secure telecommunications reception and check on him, while other passing hikers from different groups paused to assist.
Apis successfully contacted the fire department, but due to bad weather on Sunday, the rescue could not be executed immediately, despite an ambulance already waiting at the base of the mountain, Hanafi said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Saifuddin: Technical fault, not cyberattack, caused 3-hour immigration system outage
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Thousands of travellers were caught in massive queues at immigration checkpoints nationwide after a technical failure crippled the country's immigration system for more than three hours yesterday.
It forced Immigration officers to process arrivals and departures manually, with some 114 entry points nationwide affected.
Severe congestion was reported particularly at Johor's land checkpoints as thousands of Malaysians attempted to cross into Singapore for work.
The disruption, involving the Malaysian Immigration System (MyIMMs), affected major entry points across the country, including the heavily congested Johor Causeway and Second Link crossings' land checkpoints during the morning rush into Singapore.
Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said the interruption was caused by an internal technical fault and not a cybersecurity breach.
"The temporary disruption involving the MyIMMs system has been fully restored. There was no cyberattack, no data leak and no loss or damage to any information stored in the system," he told the New Straits Times today.
He said the outage disrupted Immigration inspection and processing operations for about three hours and 45 minutes before recovery works were carried out.
"Following immediate action by the Immigration Department and the relevant technical teams, the system was restored and resumed normal operations within 45 minutes," Saifuddin said.
Bewildered travellers were stranded in snaking queues at land checkpoints in Johor's land checkpoints nationwide as autogates, facial recognition systems and computerised clearance counters became unusable.
Saifuddin acknowledged that critical systems operating around the clock and handling massive transaction volumes required constant monitoring and continuous upgrades to ensure optimum stability and resilience.
He said the phased migration from the ageing Malaysian Immigration System (MyIMMs) to the new National Integrated Immigration System (MyNIISe) was already underway to enhance system capacity, operational capability and overall service delivery, with potential disruptions to existing operations having been anticipated.
He added that the Immigration Department is implementing several reinforcement measures, including upgrading storage capacity and system infrastructure, strengthening real-time monitoring mechanisms, improving recovery response plans and accelerating the MyIMMs upgrading programme to boost long-term reliability and resilience.
He also apologised on behalf of the Home Ministry for the inconvenience caused and thanked the public for their patience and cooperation.
Immigration Department director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said officers had no choice but to switch entirely to manual clearance procedures after the system breakdown yesterday.
He said all inbound and outbound travellers had to be checked manually because the e-gates could not be activated.
Zakaria said the breakdown began around 5am and lasted until 8.45am which had stemmed from technical problems at the MyIMMs data centre.
He added that the ageing MyIMMs platform remained vulnerable to recurring disruptions until it was replaced by the MyNIISe, which is expected to be fully operational by 2028.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaya Heritage eyes Nasdaq listing in 2027, US$15mil raise
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaya Heritage Holdings Ltd is eyeing a listing on the Nasdaq Stock Market in 2027 and plans to raise US$15 million (RM67.5 million) through the proposed listing to support its domestic and international expansion.
Deputy chief executive officer Oscar Woon said the company has been undergoing the Nasdaq listing process for the past six to eight months.
"We estimate the entire process will take about 18 months. By the end of the second quarter or third quarter next year, we are planning to list on Nasdaq.
"Following a successful listing there, it may become easier for us to pursue a listing on Bursa Malaysia, as the requirements are not as stringent as those for a Nasdaq listing," he told Bernama on Friday.
He said this during an event held by Sagtec Global Ltd on its acquisition of a 40 per cent stake in Malaya Heritage for US$3 million.
Sagtec Global chief executive officer Kevin Ng said Malaya Heritage's Nasdaq listing ambition is aimed at strengthening global brand recognition and promoting Malaysia's kopitiam culture internationally, while showcasing the country's multicultural identity.
He said 40 per cent of the proceeds raised will be allocated for business expansion, including the opening of new retail outlets, while 20 per cent will be channelled into Malaya Heritage's fast-moving consumer goods (FMCG) segment, including instant noodles and instant coffee products.
"Another 20 per cent will be used for working capital, and the remaining 20 per cent for marketing," he said.
Acquisition Driven by Industry Expertise
Ng said Sagtec Global, an information technology solutions provider for the food and beverage (F&B) and retail sectors, decided to acquire a restaurant business due to its strong understanding of the industry and extensive data capabilities.
He said the company originated from the F&B technology segment, with its flagship point-of-sale system having accumulated substantial backend data on restaurant operations and performance.
"This data will support Malaya Heritage's future growth, while the acquisition will also provide Sagtec Global with direct operational feedback from the restaurant business," he said.
Ng said the group plans to integrate technology into Malaya Heritage's operations, including central kitchen systems, robotics technology, and customer applications for ordering, accessing information, and providing feedback.
"We can obtain more data through the apps, similar to what is already happening in the market. But no one is doing that for a traditional kopitiam," he said.
He added that the initiative would also help transform Malaya Heritage from a traditional kopitiam business into a more structured corporate entity.
Domestic and Overseas Expansion
Meanwhile, Woon said Malaya Heritage plans to expand its restaurant network in Penang, Johor Bahru and Ipoh, Perak, over the next few years.
He said the company is focusing on flagship outlets to support its rebranding exercise and transition towards a more corporate image, while ensuring the outlets serve as benchmarks for future stores.
Currently, Malaya Heritage operates three outlets and a central kitchen across Jalan Hang Jebat, Chinatown and Cheras in Kuala Lumpur.
By the end of this year, the company plans to open four additional outlets at Pavilion Kuala Lumpur, Bukit Jalil, Damansara and Intermark Mall.
Following the completion of the acquisition, Sagtec expects Malaya Heritage to contribute about RM30 million in annual revenue over the next few years, supported by the opening of the four new outlets this year.
Ng said the planned Nasdaq listing would create further opportunities for the fast-growing Malaysian F&B group to expand into the United States and Europe, including through its FMCG business.
"Through Sagtec, we are already dealing with several distributors in the US. We are now awaiting US Food and Drug Administration (FDA) clearance, along with several certifications, before we can export our products," he said.
Ng said the company also plans to expand into Vietnam and Cambodia if the listing progresses smoothly, as both countries are viewed as fast-developing markets.
On halal certification, he said Malaya Heritage is currently undergoing the certification process and expects to obtain approval by the third quarter or fourth quarter of this year.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Strong winds, rough seas forecast for Sabah, Labuan and northern Sarawak from May 30 to June 1
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 29 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has forecast significant weather conditions in western Sabah, the Federal Territory of Labuan and northern Sarawak following the convergence of strong westerly winds expected from May 30 to June 1.
It said the conditions could result in strong winds and rough seas, as well as the formation of squall lines expected to bring wetter and windier conditions over the affected areas and waters.
“In this regard, MetMalaysia has issued a strong winds and rough seas warning for the South China Sea and the northern Straits of Melaka waters, effective until June 1,” it said in a statement today.
The public is advised to stay updated on the latest weather developments by referring to the official website at www.met.gov.my, MET Malaysia’s official social media platforms and the myCuaca application for the latest and verified information. — Bernama

Title: Anwar calls for responsible reporting in age of information overload
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 29 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has stressed the importance of responsible reporting in an era of information overload and borderless news dissemination to ensure facts are conveyed accurately, ethically and without undermining social harmony.
In a Facebook post marking the National Journalists’ Day (Hawana) today, Anwar said the role of the media extends beyond reporting the news, carrying instead a major responsibility to uphold truth with integrity, credibility, courage and accountability.
“Today, we commemorate National Journalists’ Day as a tribute to journalists and media practitioners who have long served as the voice of the people and as one of the key pillars strengthening the nation’s democracy.
“May journalists and media practitioners continue to be the voice of the people, a balance to power and the conscience of the nation,” he said.
Hawana was introduced in 2018 to recognise the contributions of journalists to national development, with May 29 chosen to mark the first publication of Utusan Melayu in 1939.
This year, Hawana carries the theme “Media Integrity Strengthens Credibility”, with the main celebration slated on June 20 in Penang to be officiated by Anwar. — Bernama

Title: Man found dead in locked car outside Penang ice cream shop
Article:
GEORGE TOWN, May 29 — A 37-year-old local man was found dead inside a car parked in front of an ice cream shop along Jalan Sungai Dua here this morning.
Timur Laut District Deputy Police Chief Supt Lim Swee Sake said police received information on the incident from members of the public at 11.47am.
He said the victim, who was from Ayer Itam, was found neatly dressed and lying in the driver’s seat of his wife’s car, which was locked from the inside.
“Investigations on the victim’s body and the surrounding area found no elements of foul play,” he said in a statement today.
Lim said police estimated that the victim had died about five days before he was discovered.
He said the body was sent to the Penang Hospital Forensic Department for a post-mortem examination, and the case has been classified as a sudden death. — Bernama

Title: Ampang, Sri Petaling LRT line runs on modified schedule after Chan Sow Lin derailment
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 29 — Light Rail Transit (LRT) stations along the Ampang/Sri Petaling Line continued to operate today on a modified service schedule following the train derailment near Chan Sow Lin Station yesterday.
A Bernama survey at several stations along the line, including from Cheras Station to Pudu Station, found that operations were running as usual with ticket counters open to the public.
To ensure the smooth movement of passengers, travel guide announcements were made periodically through the station’s loudspeaker system, in addition to Rapid Rail staff being stationed on the platforms to assist users in getting trains to their respective destinations.
At Cheras Station, only one platform was used alternately for both directions of service, involving trains heading to Putra Heights and Sentul Timur.
A passenger, Arif Norazlan, 21, said his journey from Cheras Station to Pudu Station went smoothly thanks to the assistance and guidance provided by Rapid Rail staff.
“I didn’t wait long to board the train. Although the platform used today was different from usual, the staff helped passengers a lot to board the right train,” he told Bernama.
Meanwhile, another passenger, B. Thanabala Singham, 46, said he was travelling from Masjid Jamek Station to Ampang when the staff informed him to get off at Pudu Station to use the shuttle train service to the destination.
However, he chose to continue his journey from Pudu Station using the e-hailing service.
“Following the disruption of LRT services, I decided to use e-hailing from Pudu Station to my destination. I hope that services on this route can be restored to normal as soon as possible,” he said.
In the incident yesterday morning, an Ampang/Sri Petaling Line train derailed near Chan Sow Lin Station due to a fault in the track switch which affected the service schedule.
Transport Minister Anthony Loke Siew Fook today said the affected tracks are expected to be operational by next Wednesday at the latest.
He also informed that all stations on the Ampang/Sri Petaling Line will remain operational, but will use a modified service schedule to ensure that train movements can continue during the restoration work period. — Bernama

Title: Ewon’s Upko applies to join GRS, moves to deepen Sabah coalition ties
Article:
KOTA KINABALU, May 29 — The United Progressive Kinabalu Organisation (Upko) is officially asking to become a component party of the governing Gabungan Rakyat Sabah (GRS) coalition.
Upko president Datuk Ewon Benedick led a party delegation in a meeting with Sabah chief minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor, who is also the GRS chairman, to formally hand over its application today.
“Upko hopes to contribute towards the unity and cooperation of Sabah-based parties in strengthening efforts to develop Sabah’s future as well as championing rights under the Malaysia Agreement 1963 and the Federal Constitution,” he said in a statement here today.
“The ‘Sabah First’ vision championed by Upko is aligned with GRS’ struggle through the Sabah Maju Jaya 2.0 roadmap, which must be strengthened through closer cooperation and unity among Sabah local parties,” he added.
